## Goal

Remove the beads-command-json intermediate class. Move its json slot up to beads-command-global-options so every command has the json slot by default. All commands currently deriving from beads-command-json should instead derive from beads-command.

## Current structure (lisp/beads-command.el)

beads-command-global-options  ← has actor, db, allow-stale, etc. but NO json slot
  └─ beads-command             ← inherits global options
       └─ beads-command-json   ← adds json slot (:initform t), has execute-interactive + parse methods
            └─ every concrete command (beads-command-close, beads-command-list, etc.)

## Target structure

beads-command-global-options  ← add json slot here (:initarg :json :type boolean :initform t :long-option "json" :option-type :boolean)
  └─ beads-command             ← inherits json from global-options; absorb beads-command-json methods here
       └─ every concrete command (no more beads-command-json layer)

## Changes required

1. In beads-command.el:
   - Add json slot to beads-command-global-options (same slot spec as currently in beads-command-json)
   - Move beads-command-execute-interactive method from beads-command-json to beads-command (oset cmd json nil before calling next)
   - Move beads-command-parse method from beads-command-json to beads-command (if json t, parse JSON; else call-next-method)
   - Delete the beads-command-json defclass entirely

2. In every beads-command-*.el file (and in beads-command.el examples):
   - Change (beads-defcommand foo (beads-command-json) ...) → (beads-defcommand foo (beads-command) ...)
   - Files affected: beads-command.el (doc examples), beads-command-admin.el, beads-command-blocked.el, beads-command-formula.el, beads-command-integrations.el, beads-command-restore.el, beads-command-swarm.el, beads-command-vc.el, plus many more — grep for 'beads-command-json' to find all

3. Update docstrings and comments that mention beads-command-json

## Testing

Run full test suite: BD_NO_DAEMON=1 eldev -p -dtT test
All tests must pass. Zero unexpected results.
title: Refactor: fold json slot into beads-command-global-options, remove beads-command-json

## Problem

main branch is broken after the beads-main.el → beads.el merge (commit from 2026-03-06).
33 ERT tests failing across all Emacs versions (28.2, 29.4, 30.2, snapshot).

CI run: https://github.com/r0man/beads.el/actions/runs/22755855025

## Failure 1: Duplicate function definitions

Almost every beads-command-*.el file has warnings like:
```
lisp/beads-command-admin.el:390:2: Warning: function beads-admin-cleanup used to take 0 or more arguments, now takes 0
lisp/beads-command-admin.el:390:2: Warning: function beads-admin-cleanup defined multiple times in this file
```
This means the merge added new function definitions to files that already contained those functions.
Files affected: beads-command-admin.el, beads-command-agent.el, beads-command-audit.el,
beads-command-blocked.el, beads-command-branch.el, beads-command-close.el,
beads-command-comments.el, beads-command-compact.el, beads-command-config.el,
beads-command-count.el, beads-command-create.el, beads-command-daemon.el,
beads-command-defer.el, beads-command-delete.el, beads-command-dep.el, and many more.

Fix: find and remove all duplicate definitions. Each function should appear exactly once.

## Failure 2: JSON parse error (char 9675 = ○)

Test failures like:
```
(beads-json-parse-error "Failed to parse JSON: JSON readtable error: 9675"
  :exit-code 0
  :stdout "○ btP9GVzZ-bbu ● P0 Critical feature\n○ btP9GVzZ-dsj ..."
```

The error code 9675 is the Unicode codepoint for "○" (WHITE CIRCLE). This means
`bd list` is returning human-readable text output (with ○/◐/✓ symbols) instead of JSON.
The --json flag is not being passed.

Failing tests: beads-create-test-execute-minimal-fields, beads-create-test-execute-all-fields,
beads-create-test-execute-with-dependencies, beads-create-test-preview-does-not-create-issue,
beads-delete-test-*, beads-stats-test-*, beads-types-test-integration-issue-list-by-status

These tests create issues with bd create then call bd list or similar — the list call
is not getting --json. This is likely because:
- A beads-command-list or related class got its :json slot changed/overridden by the
  duplicate definition
- Or the new beads.el defines an autoload that shadows the real implementation

## How to investigate

```bash
cd /home/roman/gt/beads_el/mayor/rig  # or polecat worktree
# Run a failing test locally:
BD_NO_DAEMON=1 eldev -p -dtT test --test beads-create-test-execute-minimal-fields

# Check for duplicate definitions:
grep -n "defun beads-admin-cleanup\|transient-define-prefix beads-close\|transient-define-suffix beads-delete" lisp/beads-command-*.el

# Check beads.el for any inline autoload forms that shadow command files:
grep -n "autoload.*beads-command\|defun beads-command" lisp/beads.el
```

## Fix approach

1. Find every "function defined multiple times" warning file
2. Remove the duplicate (keep whichever is the correct/original version)
3. Find why bd list is not getting --json — check if beads-command-list class :json slot
   is correct, check if any new code overrides it
4. Run full test suite locally: BD_NO_DAEMON=1 eldev -p -dtT test
5. All 4210 tests must pass, 0 unexpected results
6. Push fix to main

bd 0.59.0 introduced a regression (https://github.com/steveyegge/beads/issues/2395): bd list --json outputs tree-format text instead of JSON. We pinned a workaround (ba4426a: add --flat when json=t), but the fix is fragile. Use bd 0.58.0 where --json works correctly without --flat.

description:
## Context

Code review (be-pp0) identified 6 major boilerplate sources across beads command files.
This bead implements the fixes. CI must be green (be-c6j) before this work starts.

## Changes to implement

### 1. Auto-derive :cli-command from class name (~100 manual methods eliminated)

Currently every command class defines a cl-defmethod beads-command-subcommand returning
a hardcoded string. Instead, beads-defcommand should accept :cli-command "close" as a
class option and auto-generate the method, OR derive it from the class name:
  beads-command-close → "close"
  beads-command-dep-add → "dep add" (or accept :cli-command "dep add")

### 2. Default execute = run in terminal (eliminate ~130 identical execute-interactive overrides)

Most commands override execute-interactive just to call the terminal runner. The base
class beads-command-execute method should do this by default — build command line from
slots and run it. Only override for specialized display (list, show, epic, graph).

### 3. Auto-generate :global-section in the macro (eliminate ~120 separate transient calls)

beads-defcommand should auto-call beads-meta-define-transient and register global options.
Currently every command file has a separate (beads-meta-define-transient ...) call after
the class definition plus (beads-option-add-global-section ...).

### 4. Add :parse-as class option (eliminate ~13 duplicate parse methods)

Commands that return parsed issue objects all define identical parse methods. Add a
:parse-as :issue or :parse-as :issues class option to auto-generate the parser.

### 5. Auto-derive slot properties from :long-option and :type

If a slot has :long-option "title" and :type string:
- :argument defaults to "--title=" 
- :class defaults to transient-option (or transient-switch for boolean)
- :transient (description) defaults to capitalized slot name: "Title"
- :key defaults to first letter of long-option: "t"
- :initarg defaults to slot name as keyword

beads-meta.el already has SOME of this inference — make sure defcommand leverages it
fully so slots need minimal annotation.

### 6. Phase out manual infix definitions in beads-option.el

The beads-option.el file has hundreds of lines of manually-defined transient infixes
that duplicate information already in slot metadata. With (5) above, these become
redundant. The transient menu should be auto-constructed from the defclass.

## Acceptance criteria

- A typical command definition is ~10-15 lines (class + slots), not 50-100+
- No manual beads-meta-define-transient calls needed at command definition sites
- No manual beads-command-subcommand methods needed
- No manual execute-interactive overrides for simple run-in-terminal commands
- All existing tests pass (3675/3675)
- Byte-compilation clean

## Files to modify

- lisp/beads-command.el — enhance macro, add default methods
- lisp/beads-meta.el — improve inference defaults
- lisp/beads-option.el — simplify or phase out manual infixes
- lisp/beads-command-*.el — migrate to new shorter form (incremental, start with 5-10 commands as proof)

## Dependencies

- be-c6j must be merged first (CI fix)
notes: Design analysis complete. 3 key changes: (1) auto-derive subcommand from class name, (2) default execute-interactive sets json=nil for terminal, (3) auto-generate transient from beads-defcommand :global-section. Also :cli-command option for non-standard names.
title: Implement beads-defcommand smart defaults to eliminate ~3800 lines of boilerplate

CRITICAL: Tests are creating bt* databases on the production Dolt server (port 3307), causing server overload, i/o timeouts, and read-only mode. This has caused multiple outages.

## Requirements

1. Every ERT test run MUST spawn its own dedicated Dolt server on a random free port
2. Tests MUST NEVER connect to port 3307 or any pre-existing Dolt server
3. The test Dolt server should use a temporary data directory (e.g. make-temp-file)
4. The test Dolt server MUST be torn down after tests complete (even on failure — use unwind-protect)
5. All bd commands in tests must be pointed at the test server via environment or --db flag

## Implementation approach

Create a test helper (e.g. beads-test-with-dolt macro or fixture) that:
1. Finds a free port: (let ((port (beads-test--find-free-port))) ...)
2. Creates temp data dir: (make-temp-file "beads-test-dolt" t)
3. Starts dolt sql-server on that port with that data dir
4. Sets environment/config so all bd commands use this server
5. Runs the test body
6. Tears down the server and cleans up temp dir in unwind-protect

## Example
```elisp
(defmacro beads-test-with-dolt (&rest body)
  "Run BODY with an isolated Dolt server on a random free port."
  (declare (indent 0))
  \`(let* ((port (beads-test--find-free-port))
          (data-dir (make-temp-file "beads-test-dolt" t))
          (process (beads-test--start-dolt port data-dir)))
     (unwind-protect
         (let ((process-environment
                (cons (format "BD_DOLT_PORT=%d" port)
                      (cons (format "BD_DATA_DIR=%s" data-dir)
                            process-environment))))
           ,@body)
       (beads-test--stop-dolt process)
       (delete-directory data-dir t))))
```

## Root cause
Current tests either use no Dolt isolation or connect to the production server on 3307. The bt* prefix databases they create accumulate (126+ observed) and overload the server.

## Test scenarios

### 1. Project setup & basic workflow
- Create a temp git repo + bd init with a test prefix
- Use M-x beads to open the main transient menu
- Create issues via beads-create transient (different types: task, bug, feature)
- List issues via beads-list (verify tabulated-list display)
- Show issue details via beads-show
- Update issues (change status, priority, assignee)
- Close issues with reason
- Reopen closed issues
- Delete issues

### 2. Epic & dependency management
- Create an epic with child issues
- Add dependencies between issues (bd dep add)
- View dependency graph (beads-graph)
- Check blocked issues (beads-blocked)
- Check ready issues (beads-ready)
- View epic status (beads-epic)

### 3. Agent workflows
- Spawn an agent from the main beads project repo
- Spawn an agent on an existing worktree
- Spawn an agent on a new worktree (beads-worktree-create)
- Verify agents can create/update/close issues
- Verify agent experience is consistent across main repo and worktrees
- Test beads-agent-menu transient

### 4. Interactive command quality audit
For each command accessible via M-x beads, evaluate:
- Does it work? (no errors on invocation)
- Is the UX appropriate? (e.g., list commands should use tabulated-list-mode,
  not raw terminal output)
- Commands that SHOULD have rich interactive display:
  - beads-list → tabulated-list (already done?)
  - beads-ready → tabulated-list
  - beads-blocked → tabulated-list
  - beads-stale → tabulated-list
  - beads-search → tabulated-list results
  - beads-show → formatted detail buffer
  - beads-epic → tree view
  - beads-graph → graphviz image or ASCII
  - beads-stats → formatted summary buffer
  - beads-activity → log-like buffer
  - beads-history → log-view-mode or similar
  - beads-comments → threaded view
  - beads-diff → diff-mode buffer
- Commands where terminal output is fine:
  - beads-create, beads-update, beads-close (action commands)
  - beads-init, beads-doctor, beads-sync (admin commands)

### 5. Worktree integration
- Create a worktree via beads-worktree-create
- Verify beads commands work from inside the worktree
- Verify .beads discovery works (worktree → main repo fallback)
- Remove worktree via beads-worktree-remove

### 6. Labels, state, comments
- Add/remove labels
- Set state on issues
- Add comments to issues
- View comments

## Deliverable

- File beads issues under THIS epic for every bug, UX issue, or improvement found
- Categorize findings: bug (broken), UX (works but poor experience), feature (missing)
- Include reproduction steps and expected vs actual behavior
- Prioritize: P0 = broken/crashes, P1 = bad UX, P2 = nice-to-have improvements

## Technical approach

Use the tmux skill to:
1. Start Emacs in a tmux session with beads.el loaded (use the test init.el or configure load-path)
2. Drive Emacs via keystroke sequences
3. Capture buffer contents to verify output
4. Use M-x eval-expression for programmatic checks when needed

The test Emacs should use the beads_el rig's init.el at:
  share/emacs.d/init.el
Or configure load-path to point at lisp/ directly.

design:
## Design: Search results in tabulated-list-mode

### Problem
beads-search currently uses the default beads-command-json execute-interactive
which sets json=nil and runs in a terminal/compilation buffer. Results should
display in tabulated-list-mode like beads-list for consistent navigation.

### Approach
1. Add :parse-as :issues to beads-command-search so JSON results parse to beads-issue objects
2. Override beads-command-execute-interactive for beads-command-search to:
   - Execute with JSON enabled (keep json=t, NOT call the beads-command-json method)
   - Parse results into beads-issue objects
   - Display in beads-list-mode buffer using beads-list--populate-buffer
3. Add beads-buffer-list support for "search" buffer type
4. Reuse all existing beads-list infrastructure (mode, keymap, faces, formatting)

### Buffer naming
*beads-search[PROJECT]*  or *beads-search[PROJECT] query*

### Key decisions
- Reuse beads-list-mode (same columns, faces, keymap) - search is just filtered list
- Store search command in beads-list--command as 'search symbol
- Support refresh (g key) by re-executing the search
- Use existing beads-list--get-or-create-buffer pattern with 'search type
title: UX: beads-search results should use tabulated-list-mode

description: M-x beads-activity (or beads-command-activity\!) fails with 'unknown command activity for bd'. The bd CLI does not have an activity subcommand. Either the feature was removed from bd, or the Emacs command references a planned-but-unimplemented CLI command.
design:
## Design: Remove beads-command-activity module

### Problem
beads-command-activity.el defines an EIEIO command class for `bd activity` — a CLI subcommand that does not exist. Running M-x beads-activity fails with 'unknown command activity for bd'.

### Analysis
- `bd activity` is not in `bd --help` output (neither top-level nor subcommand)
- The closest related commands are:
  - `bd mol last-activity <mol-id>` — returns a single timestamp
  - `bd history <id>` — shows version history of an issue
  - `bd status` — shows overview/stats including recent activity
- The activity module was generated for a planned-but-never-implemented CLI command

### Fix: Remove the dead module entirely
1. Delete `lisp/beads-command-activity.el` (the command class)
2. Delete `lisp/test/beads-command-activity-test.el` (its tests)
3. Remove the 'a' keybinding from `beads-main.el` line 165

### Why not stub/redirect?
- No equivalent `bd` command exists to redirect to
- `bd mol last-activity` is a single-issue query, not a feed
- Better to remove dead code than leave a misleading menu entry
- If `bd activity` is added later, the Emacs command can be re-implemented
title: Bug: beads-activity calls non-existent bd activity command

```
## Problem

beads.el integration tests still create orphan databases on the production Dolt server (port 3307). The be-9ra fix used BD_NO_DAEMON=1 (SQLite fallback) but:
1. Integration tests that exercise the Dolt backend still connect to port 3307
2. The prefix was supposed to change from 'beadsTest' to 'bt' but beads-integration-test.el line 83 still generates 'beadsTest' prefixes
3. We cleaned up 11+ orphan bt*/beadsTest* DBs just today — they keep accumulating

## Root Cause

beads-integration-test.el:beads-test--generate-unique-prefix still generates 'beadsTest' + 6 random chars and connects to the shared production server.

## Required Solution

### Test-local Dolt server
The test harness must start a fresh `dolt sql-server` on a dedicated port (NOT 3307) for the test run:

1. **Find a free port** at test startup (e.g., port 0 binding trick or fixed test port like 3308)
2. **Start dolt** on that port: `start-process ... "dolt" "sql-server" "--port" port-str`
3. **Configure each test repo** to use that port after bd init:
   - `bd dolt set port <N>` writes port to .beads/metadata.json
   - bd then connects to port N instead of 3307
4. **Stop the dolt process** on teardown (unwind-protect) — killing the process drops all DBs automatically
5. **No cleanup needed** — dolt server death = all test databases gone

### Implementation plan

In beads-integration-test.el:

```
defvar beads-test--dolt-server-process nil
defvar beads-test--dolt-server-port nil
defvar beads-test--dolt-server-data-dir nil

defun beads-test--find-free-port ()
  ;; bind to port 0, get assigned port, close socket
  
defun beads-test--start-test-dolt-server ()
  ;; find free port
  ;; make-temp-file data-dir
  ;; start-process dolt sql-server --port N --data-dir D
  ;; wait for server to be ready (retry connect)
  ;; store process/port/data-dir in vars

defun beads-test--stop-test-dolt-server ()
  ;; kill the process
  ;; delete data-dir
  ;; nil out the vars

defun beads-test--init-beads (dir &optional prefix quiet)
  ;; after bd init, run: bd dolt set port beads-test--dolt-server-port
  ;; ensures this repo never touches 3307

;; Eldev hook or ert-runner hook:
;; (add-hook 'ert-runner-before-suite-hook #'beads-test--start-test-dolt-server)
;; (add-hook 'ert-runner-after-suite-hook #'beads-test--stop-test-dolt-server)
;; OR use Eldev test prepare/teardown hooks
```

### Also fix
- beads-test--generate-unique-prefix: change 'beadsTest' prefix to 'bt' (was supposed to be done in be-9ra but wasn't)
- Remove beads-test--drop-dolt-database — no longer needed (server death cleans up)

## Acceptance criteria
- No bt* or beadsTest* databases ever appear on the production server (port 3307) after running eldev test
- Integration tests that test Dolt backend still pass (they now use port N)
- Tests that don't need Dolt continue to use BD_NO_DAEMON=1 (SQLite)
- eldev -p -dtT test passes
- gt dolt status shows zero orphans after a full test run

## Key files
- lisp/test/beads-integration-test.el — main fix location
- lisp/test/beads-test.el — may also need updates
- Eldev — test lifecycle hooks if needed
title: Test harness: dedicated Dolt server on separate port, never touch port 3307

## Problem

beads.el tests currently talk to the live Gas Town Dolt server on port 3307. This causes:
1. Test database accumulation (beadsTest* with random suffixes) polluting production
2. Dolt instability from memory pressure (30+ orphan test DBs caused repeated crashes)
3. Ghost workspace directories at the town root tricking gt dolt status

## Requirements

### 1. Separate test Dolt server
- Tests MUST run against a dedicated Dolt instance on a DIFFERENT port (e.g., 3308 or ephemeral)
- Tests MUST NEVER connect to port 3307 (the live Gas Town server)
- The test server should be started/stopped by the test harness (or test setup/teardown)

### 2. Consistent test database naming
- All test databases should use a uniform prefix (e.g., `beads_test_` or `bt_`) so they're easily identifiable
- No more random suffixes like beadsTest4fzcfJ — use a predictable pattern

### 3. Guaranteed cleanup
- Tests MUST clean up after themselves, whether they pass or fail
- Use unwind-protect, teardown hooks, or similar to ensure databases are dropped
- If using a temporary Dolt server, stopping it cleans everything

### 4. No workspace directory pollution
- Tests should NOT create directories at the Gas Town root (/home/roman/gt/)
- Test workspaces should live in a temp dir or under the rig's test directory

## Context
- Current test infrastructure is in the beads.el codebase
- The Dolt connection config is likely in beads' backend/server code
- Look at how tests currently create databases and connect
- This has caused 3 separate Dolt instability incidents (hq-oob, hq-wyss, hq-hekq)
notes: Design: (1) Global BD_NO_DAEMON=1 enforcement at test load time in beads-test.el + beads-integration-test.el + Eldev; (2) Standardize prefix to 'bt' instead of 'beadsTest'; (3) Fix early beads-command-test.el init tests to use macros; (4) Ghost workspace pollution eliminated by enforcing no-daemon mode.

description:
## Bug

'bd list --json' ignores the --json flag and outputs text format instead of JSON.

## Reproduction

  cd /home/roman/gt
  bd list --type=convoy --status=open --json

Expected: JSON array output like 'bd show --json'
Actual: Text table format starting with '○ hq-cv-...' symbols, or 'No issues found.' for empty results

## Impact

'gt convoy list/check/stranded' and 'gt compact' all fail with:
  Error: parsing convoy list: invalid character 'â' looking for beginning of value

The first byte of '○' (U+25CB) is 0xE2, which appears as 'â' in the JSON parse error.

## Root Cause

The PersistentPreRun in main.go checks:
  if !cmd.Flags().Changed('json') {
      jsonOutput = config.GetBool('json')
  }

This may not work correctly for the list subcommand — the 'jsonOutput' global stays false even when --json is passed. The list command at line 717 checks 'if jsonOutput' and skips JSON output.

## Workaround

gt convoy was unblocked by closing all completed convoys. With no open convoys, the JSON parse error on 'No issues found.' appears but empty arrays [] would also be acceptable.

## Note

'bd show --json' works correctly (returns proper JSON). Only 'bd list --json' is affected.
title: bd list --json doesn't output JSON: ignores --json flag, outputs text format

## Problem

After merging beads-main.el into beads.el, `M-x beads` may fail with:

  transient-setup: Suffix command beads-list is not defined or autoloaded

Root cause: beads.el defines the transient menu referencing command symbols (beads-list, beads-show, beads-create, etc.) but these symbols live in separate command files. Without a package-generated autoloads file being loaded first, `(require 'beads)` does not make these symbols available at menu setup time.

## Fix

1. **Explicit autoloads in beads.el**: Add `(autoload 'beads-list "beads-command-list" nil t)` etc. for every symbol referenced in the transient menu. Do NOT use `(require ...)` for command namespaces — use autoloads only.

2. **beads-defcommand macro**: Ensure the macro (or its generated interactive function) has a `;;;###autoload` cookie so that package autoload generation picks it up. The interactive wrapper function name (e.g. `beads-list`) must be autoloadable.

3. **Remove any (require 'beads-command-*) calls** from beads.el — replace with explicit `(autoload ...)` forms so the file stays lazily loadable.

## Acceptance criteria

- `emacs -nw -Q -L <lisp-dir> --eval "(require 'beads)" --eval "(call-interactively #'beads)"` opens the transient menu without any error
- All existing ERT tests pass: `eldev -p -dtT test`
- Zero byte-compile warnings: `eldev -p -dtT compile`
- Zero lint errors: `eldev -p -dtT lint`
- Live tmux acceptance test: open `emacs -nw` in tmux, `M-x beads`, menu appears, navigate submenus, no errors

## Notes

- Parallel fix to ge-wgg (gastown.el same issue)
- CI is currently red on main (be-09v) — fix this issue on a fresh branch, do not depend on be-09v
- Use TDD: write a test that loads beads.el in isolation and calls beads transient, verify no error before implementing
title: Fix: M-x beads fails — suffix commands not autoloaded, beads-defcommand macro must emit autoloads

## Failing tests (all Emacs versions)

- beads-coverage-boost-compact-stats-execute-interactive
- beads-coverage-boost-compact-prune-execute-interactive
- beads-coverage-boost-compact-purge-execute-interactive

File: lisp/test/beads-coverage-boost-test.el, lines 170-192

## Root cause

Tests stub cl-call-next-method with cl-letf:
  (cl-letf (((symbol-function 'cl-call-next-method) (lambda (&rest _) nil)))
    (beads-command-execute-interactive cmd))

cl-call-next-method is NOT a regular symbol in compiled EIEIO methods — it compiles to cl--cnm bytecode. cl-letf cannot intercept it. The real next method fires, calls beads-command--run-in-terminal, which creates a term-mode buffer. erase-buffer fails with (buffer-read-only) error.

## Fix

description:
## Problem

Main branch CI is red — 35 test failures introduced by commit 0413caf (beads.el-0wf: remove redundant eval-and-compile wrappers).

Two failure modes:

1. **Dep tests (11 failures)**: \`invalid-slot-name :depends-on\` in beads-command-dep-add.
   The slot isn't being defined because the class definition isn't available at compile time.
   Obsidian stripped eval-and-compile from defclass calls, but some classes use raw
   defclass (not beads-defcommand macro) and STILL NEED eval-and-compile.

2. **List tests (24 failures)**: \`(beads-command-list-p obj)\` returns nil.
   Class identity mismatch — the class was defined at load time but the type predicate
   was compiled against a different class object. Classic eval-and-compile issue.

## Root cause

The beads.el-0wf commit assumed ALL defclass calls go through beads-defcommand (which
wraps in eval-and-compile internally). But some classes use raw defclass and had their
eval-and-compile wrappers incorrectly removed.

## Fix

1. Identify which defclass calls in the failing files DON'T use beads-defcommand
2. Restore eval-and-compile for those specific raw defclass calls
3. Verify: all 3675 tests pass, CI goes green

## Failing tests

Dep: beads-command-test-dep-add-basic, beads-command-test-dep-add-command-line-basic,
beads-command-test-dep-add-command-line-with-type, beads-command-test-dep-add-validation-*,
beads-command-test-dep-add-with-type, beads-command-test-dep-remove-*, 
beads-command-test-unit-list-instantiation-defaults

List: beads-list-test-parse-transient-args-* (24 tests)

## Files

- lisp/beads-command-dep.el
- lisp/beads-command-list.el
- Possibly others that use raw defclass without beads-defcommand

## CI run

gh run view 22548983407
title: Fix 35 CI test failures from eval-and-compile removal (beads.el-0wf regression)

## Goal

Invert the current relationship between beads-command and beads-command-global-options so that beads-command is the minimal abstract base, beads-command-global-options extends it (adding all CLI global option slots), and every concrete command inherits from beads-command-global-options.

## Current hierarchy (origin/main)

    beads-command-global-options ()           ← standalone mixin with global slots (json, actor, db, …)
      └─ beads-command (beads-command-global-options)  ← base class, inherits all option slots
           └─ every concrete command (beads-command)   ← inherits from beads-command

## Target hierarchy

    beads-command ()                          ← minimal abstract base (execution machinery, generics only, no option slots)
      └─ beads-command-global-options (beads-command)  ← inherits base, adds all global option slots
           └─ every concrete command (beads-command-global-options)  ← inherits full options

## Changes in beads-command.el

1. Change beads-command-global-options superclass: () → (beads-command)
2. Change beads-command superclass: (beads-command-global-options) → ()
   - Remove all global option slots from beads-command (they live in beads-command-global-options)
   - Keep only the execution machinery and generic method definitions
3. Update all docstrings/comments that describe the hierarchy

## Changes in every beads-command-*.el (53 files)

Every occurrence of:
  (beads-defcommand foo (beads-command) …)
must become:
  (beads-defcommand foo (beads-command-global-options) …)

description:
When running M-x gastown, Emacs errors: 'transient-setup: Suffix command gastown-status is not defined or autoloaded.' The root cause: beads-defcommand generates transient commands but does not emit autoload cookies for them. The main transient menus in beads and gastown then require those modules to be loaded, which defeats lazy loading. Fix: beads-defcommand should generate autoloads for the autogenerated transient commands, so the top-level transient menus (beads, gastown) can reference them as autoloaded symbols without requiring the defining modules.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: Tests must be run inside tmux using a fresh non-graphical Emacs (emacs -Q -nw or emacs --batch). Verify that: (1) autoloads are generated for beads-defcommand transient commands, (2) M-x gastown works without pre-requiring the defining modules, (3) M-x beads works the same way. Test in a clean tmux session with emacs -nw to confirm no graphical dependencies.
notes: Design: Modify beads-defcommand and beads-meta-define-transient to emit (autoload ...) forms in their expansion. For beads-defcommand with :global-section, derive transient name and file name at macroexpansion time and emit autoload form. For beads-meta-define-transient, compute prefix-sym and file similarly. Add condition-case for graceful fallback when class not available during autoload generation. Add Eldev hook to load macros during autoload generation so ;;;###autoload before macro calls works.
title: beads-defcommand should generate autoloads for autogenerated transient commands

title: Pre-existing: Dolt server in read-only mode caused integration test failures on main

description:
## Problem

When running beads.el tests locally, bd init auto-discovers the Gas Town production
Dolt server on port 3307 and creates test databases there. This causes:
1. Orphan beadsTestXXXXXX databases accumulating in production (~41 found recently)
2. Server contention causing i/o timeouts during batch test runs
3. Refinery merge failures from non-deterministic Dolt flakes

CI avoids this by running with BD_NO_DAEMON=1 (no shared server), but local test
runs always hit the production server.

## Root cause

beads-test-create-project (test/beads-test.el:40-63) calls beads-command-init without
specifying a server port. bd init auto-discovers port 3307 and creates databases on
the production Gas Town server.

## Solution

Run tests against an isolated Dolt server on a different port.

### Implementation

1. Add a beads-test-server-port variable (defvar) that defaults to an env var
   BEADS_TEST_PORT or falls back to a non-production port (e.g., 3308)

2. Update beads-test-create-project to pass :server-port to beads-command-init,
   using beads-test-server-port

3. Add test setup/teardown that:
   - Starts a dedicated Dolt server on beads-test-server-port with a temp data-dir
     (e.g., /tmp/beads-test-dolt-XXXXX) before the test suite runs
   - Stops the server and cleans up after the suite finishes
   - OR: detect if a test server is already running on that port and reuse it

4. Update the Makefile/Eldev test target to auto-start the test server if needed

5. Update .github/workflows/test.yml if needed (CI currently uses BD_NO_DAEMON=1
   which may still work, but verify)

6. Update beads-test-with-project teardown to DROP DATABASE on the test server
   (defense in depth — even if using isolated server, clean up after each test)

### Key files

- lisp/test/beads-test.el — test helper infrastructure
- lisp/test/beads-integration-test.el — integration test helpers (same pattern)
- lisp/beads-command-init.el — needs :server-port slot if not present
- .github/workflows/test.yml — CI workflow
- Eldev — build/test configuration

## Acceptance criteria

- All tests pass locally (3675/3675) WITHOUT touching the production Dolt server on 3307
- All tests pass on CI (GitHub Actions)
- No orphan databases created in production .dolt-data/
- Running tests locally while Gas Town is active causes zero interference
design: Design: Use BD_NO_DAEMON=1 in process-environment within test infrastructure macros/functions. This matches CI behavior exactly and prevents ALL test bd commands from touching the production Dolt server. Also add server-port/host/user slots to beads-command-init for completeness. Key changes: beads-test.el (add beads-test-no-daemon defvar, update create-project/with-project/with-shared-project), beads-integration-test.el (same pattern), beads-command-init.el (add server connection slots).
title: Isolate test Dolt server from production to prevent orphan database pollution

description:
## Problem

1. `M-x beads` (beads-main.el) hardcodes ~80 requires and manually lists every command. Adding a new command means editing beads-main.el, adding a require, adding an autoload in beads.el, and wiring up the transient entry — way too much boilerplate.

2. `beads-defcommand` macro is too thin — it only generates defclass + a `NAME!` bang function. It should do much more to eliminate boilerplate:
   - Wrap in `eval-and-compile` so the class is available at macro-expansion time
   - Generate `;;;###autoload` cookies for the interactive transient command (e.g., `beads-close`)
   - Generate the autoload form in beads.el automatically (or make it unnecessary via autoload cookies)

3. The class hierarchy is flat and doesn't match `bd` CLI structure:
   - `beads-command` (base) → `beads-command-json` → concrete commands
   - Missing: `beads-command-global-options` layer that inherits from `beads-command` and adds standard global flags (--actor, --db, --json, etc.)
   - Concrete command classes should inherit from the right intermediate class

## Requirements

### 1. Refactor `beads-defcommand` macro

The macro should:
- Wrap class definition in `eval-and-compile`
- Auto-generate the transient prefix command (the interactive `beads-FOO` function) from slot metadata
- Generate `;;;###autoload` cookies for the interactive command
- Generate the `NAME!` programmatic convenience function
- Accept `:cli-command "close"` to specify the bd subcommand name
- Accept `:transient-key "x"` and `:transient-description "Close issue"` for the parent menu entry

Goal: defining a new command class should be a single `beads-defcommand` form that produces everything needed — class, transient menu, autoload, bang function.

### 2. Rethink class hierarchy

```
beads-command                      ; bare base (executable, result slot)
├── beads-command-global-options   ; adds --actor, --db, --json, --no-daemon etc.
│   ├── beads-command-create       ; concrete
│   ├── beads-command-close        ; concrete
│   ├── beads-command-list         ; concrete (specialized execute for tabulated display)
│   ├── beads-command-show         ; concrete (specialized execute for detail view)
│   └── ...
└── beads-command-init             ; special: no global options needed
```

### 3. Rewrite `beads-main.el` transient menu

- Should NOT require any `beads-command-*.el` modules
- Should only reference autoloaded symbols (e.g., `beads-close`, `beads-list`)
- Organize groups to mirror `bd --help` categories:
  - Working With Issues (create, close, update, show, edit, list, delete, reopen, search, move, refile, promote, q, query)
  - Views & Reports (count, diff, find-duplicates, history, lint, stale, status, types)
  - Dependencies & Structure (dep, duplicate, duplicates, epic, graph, supersede, swarm)
  - Sync & Data (backup, branch, export, federation, restore, sync, vc)
  - Setup & Configuration (config, dolt, hooks, human, info, init, kv, onboard, prime, quickstart, setup, where)
  - Maintenance (compact, doctor, rename-prefix)
  - Labels & State (label, set-state, state)
  - Agent & Workflow (agent, formula, gate, merge-slot, mol, slot)
  - Integrations (commented-out placeholders for future: jira, linear, repo, mail)

### 4. Make it trivial to add new commands from `bd CMD --help`

A developer should be able to:
1. Run `bd close --help`
2. Map flags to slot properties using beads-meta conventions
3. Write a single `beads-defcommand` form
4. Get a working transient menu, autoload, and programmatic API

For most commands, the default `beads-command-execute` (run bd, parse JSON) is sufficient. Only commands needing special display (list → tabulated-list, show → detail buffer, epic → tree view, graph → graphviz) need a specialized execute method.

## Files to modify

- `lisp/beads-command.el` — refactor macro, class hierarchy
- `lisp/beads-main.el` — rewrite to use autoloads only, mirror bd --help
- `lisp/beads.el` — remove manual autoloads that the macro now generates
- Various `lisp/beads-command-*.el` — migrate to new macro API (can be incremental)

## Testing

- `M-x beads` should show the full menu with all commands reachable
- Each command in the menu should work (autoload triggers, transient opens)
- Byte-compilation should produce no warnings
- Existing tests should still pass
notes: Analysis: Only beads-command-close uses beads-defcommand-with-transient. All other ~100 commands use plain beads-defcommand. beads-main.el has ~80 requires and manually lists every command. Class hierarchy: beads-command-global-options (mixin) -> beads-command (abstract base) -> beads-command-json (adds json support). Design: 1) Enhance beads-defcommand to wrap in eval-and-compile, generate autoload cookies 2) beads-main.el should only use autoloaded symbols, no requires of command modules 3) Keep existing beads-defcommand-with-transient for commands with rich slot metadata
title: Refactor beads-defcommand macro and implement full transient menu

## Goal

## What to add / change

### 1. Dedicated Dolt server section (NEW)

Add a section early in the file (before or in "Build and Test Commands"):

```
## Development Dolt Server

beads.el development and testing uses a **dedicated Dolt server on port 3308**,
completely isolated from the Gas Town production server (port 3307).

### Start the dev Dolt server

Before running integration tests or bd commands in dev, start the dedicated server:

```bash
# Start Dolt on port 3308 (beads.el dev port)
dolt sql-server --port 3308 --data-dir ~/.dolt-beads-dev &
DOLT_PID=$\!

# Or use bd dolt start after configuring port in your test repo:
# bd dolt set port 3308
```

Configure your test/dev beads repos to use port 3308:
```bash
cd /path/to/your/test-repo
bd dolt set port 3308
```

### NEVER touch port 3307

Port 3307 is the live Gas Town production Dolt server. Tests and development
work MUST NEVER connect to it. Always verify you are on port 3308 before
running bd commands that touch Dolt.

### Stop the dev server

```bash
kill $DOLT_PID
# Or: bd dolt stop  (in a repo configured for port 3308)
```
```

### 2. TDD workflow (ADD to Development Workflow section)

Make the TDD loop explicit. After the step "Edit source files", add:

```
### TDD Loop (MANDATORY)

For every code change, follow this cycle strictly:
1. **Write the test first** — add ERT test to the relevant test file
2. **Run tests — they should FAIL** (red): `BD_NO_DAEMON=1 eldev -p -dtT test`
3. **Implement the minimum code** to make the test pass
4. **Run tests — they should PASS** (green): `BD_NO_DAEMON=1 eldev -p -dtT test`
5. **Refactor** if needed, keeping tests green
6. **Repeat** for next change

Never write implementation before writing the test. If a test is hard to write,
that is a signal the design needs simplification.
```

### 3. Acceptance testing in tmux (SHARPEN existing section)

The existing section is good but add:
- How to start a full emacs -nw session in tmux (not just emacsclient)
- Explicit example of driving M-x beads and navigating the menu
- How to use the tmux skill in Claude Code to control the session

Add this to the Acceptance Testing section:

```
### Using the tmux skill

In Claude Code, use the tmux skill to drive the Emacs session:
```
/tmux
```

This allows you to send keystrokes and capture pane output programmatically.
Drive the full user workflow: open menus, navigate transients, verify output.

Research all remaining boilerplate patterns across both repos and implement
macro improvements to eliminate them. This is a design-first task — read the
code thoroughly before writing anything.

== KNOWN GAPS TO INVESTIGATE ==

1. gastown-defcommand is missing features beads-defcommand already has

beads-defcommand supports:
  :cli-command STR     -> generates cl-defmethod gastown-command-subcommand
  :global-section SYM  -> generates transient menu via beads-meta-define-transient
                          + generates autoload form
  :parse-as :issue/:issues -> generates beads-command-parse method

gastown-defcommand has NONE of these. Every gastown command file therefore has:
  - A hand-written cl-defmethod gastown-command-subcommand returning a literal string
  - A separate beads-meta-define-transient call + manual autoload cookie
  - Hand-written gastown-command-parse overrides for commands that need data

Proposed: add :cli-command, :global-section (using gastown-option-global-section),
and :parse-as (for JSON->alist, JSON->list-of-alists) to gastown-defcommand,
mirroring beads-defcommand.

2. CLI subcommand auto-derivation

Both macros currently require an explicit :cli-command string. But the subcommand
name is almost always derivable from the class name by stripping the prefix:
  beads-command-close        -> "close"
  gastown-command-status     -> "status"
  gastown-command-rig-list   -> "rig list" (check: may need "rig" subcommand nesting)

Investigate: can :cli-command be made optional with auto-derivation as the default?
Check multi-level subcommands (e.g. gastown-command-convoy-list -> "convoy list").

3. Validation boilerplate

Many commands have near-identical cl-defmethod beads-command-validate bodies:
  - Check required slots are non-nil/non-empty
  - Validate string-list contents
Consider a :required-slots (slot1 slot2 ...) macro option that auto-generates
the validate method for the common case.

4. execute-interactive boilerplate in beads.el

Some beads commands override beads-command-execute-interactive only to:
  - Call beads-command-execute
  - Display a message with the result
  - Invalidate the completion cache

Consider a :on-success option or a default execute-interactive that calls a
user-supplied display function, reducing explicit method overrides.

5. Shared infrastructure

gastown already requires beads-meta and uses beads-meta-define-transient.
Can gastown-defcommand delegate option extraction to beads helpers
(beads--extract-option etc.) to avoid duplicating that logic?

== APPROACH ==

1. Read ALL command files in both repos carefully. Catalog every pattern that
   appears more than twice and estimate the line-count reduction per pattern.
2. Design the macro changes. Prioritise the highest-impact changes.
3. Implement in beads.el first (since gastown depends on it), then gastown.el.
4. For each change, migrate existing commands to use the new form.
5. Verify no regressions: BD_NO_DAEMON=1 eldev -p -dtT test (beads), eldev test (gastown).

== SUCCESS CRITERIA ==

A typical simple command (one subcommand, standard JSON output, basic transient)
should be fully defined by a single beads-defcommand / gastown-defcommand call
with no separate method definitions required.

description:
## Task

Code review of the current beads-defcommand macro and command class definitions.
Produce actionable suggestions for reducing boilerplate further.

## Guiding principles

1. **Good defaults**: Most options should not need to be declared twice. If the slot
   is named :title with :long-option "title", the transient key, description, argument
   format, etc. should all be derivable automatically. Don't make the user spell out
   what can be inferred.

2. **Single declaration**: Most commands should work out of the box with just:
   ```elisp
   ;;;###autoload
   (beads-defcommand beads-command-foo (beads-command-global-options)
     ((name :initarg :name :type string :long-option "name")
      (force :initarg :force :type boolean :long-option "force"))
     :cli-command "foo"
     :documentation "Run bd foo.")
   ```
   And get: class, transient menu with infixes, interactive command, bang function,
   autoload — all working.

3. **Default execute = run in terminal**: The default beads-command-execute method
   should build the command line from slots and run it. No need to override execute
   for most commands. Only specialize for commands needing custom display (list →
   tabulated-list, show → detail buffer, epic → tree view, graph → graphviz).

4. **Default transient from defclass**: The transient menu should be auto-constructed
   from slot metadata (:long-option, :type, :key, etc.). If a slot has :long-option,
   it automatically becomes a transient infix. Boolean slots become switches, string
   slots become options. Keys can be auto-assigned from first letter of option name.

## Deliverable

A review document (as bead notes or comments) with:
- Current boilerplate inventory: what's repeated across command files
- Specific suggestions with before/after code examples
- Which defaults should be added to beads-defcommand
- Which slot properties can be auto-derived (e.g., :argument from :long-option,
  :transient description from slot :documentation, :class from :type)
- Edge cases that need explicit configuration vs the 90% case that should be zero-config

## Scope

Read the current code in:
- lisp/beads-command.el (macro + base classes)
- lisp/beads-meta.el (slot property infrastructure)  
- lisp/beads-option.el (transient generation)
- A representative sample of command files (close, create, list, show, dep, epic)
- Compare with bd CLI --help output for those commands

Do NOT modify code — this is a review/analysis task only.
title: Code review: reduce beads-defcommand boilerplate with better defaults

## Goal

The :json slot in beads-command-global-options currently defaults to t.
This means every command passes --json to bd by default, even interactive
ones run from the transient menu where a human wants colored text output.

The current workaround is an (oset command json nil) call in
beads-command-execute-interactive. This is a hack. Fix it properly.

## Changes

### 1. beads-command.el — flip default

In beads-command-global-options, change:
  (json ... :initform t ...)
to:
  (json ... :initform nil ...)

### 2. beads-command.el — remove the oset hack

In beads-command-execute-interactive the line:
  (oset command json nil)
was only needed because the default was t. Remove it.
The docstring referencing this should be updated too.

### 3. Audit: mark commands that need JSON output

Commands called programmatically (not via execute-interactive) that parse
their output as structured data need (json :initarg :json :initform t) in
their slot definition, OR must be instantiated with :json t at the call site.

Strategy: grep for all (beads-defcommand ... :parse-as ...) — these always
need json because their generated beads-command-parse method only works when
json is t. Add a :json slot override with :initform t to each of these, OR
set :json t at every call site.

Also grep for direct beads-command-execute calls (not execute-interactive)
to find programmatic callers that rely on JSON parsing — ensure those pass
:json t.

The completion system (beads-completion.el) fetches issue lists
programmatically — check that those command instantiations include :json t.

### 4. Update docstrings

Update the class and method docstrings that describe the old default behaviour.

## Testing

BD_NO_DAEMON=1 eldev -p -dtT test
All tests pass. Interactive M-x beads commands show human-readable output
(no --json flag in the built command). List views and completion still
work (they pass --json via explicit :json t).
title: Change :json default to nil; commands that need data must set :json t explicitly

description:
## Problem

beads.el tests create Dolt databases on the shared server (port 3307) via `bd init` with unique `beadsTestXXXXXX` prefixes, but the teardown in `beads-test-with-project` (beads-test.el:150-151) only deletes the temp filesystem directory — it never issues a DROP DATABASE to remove the database from the Dolt server.

Result: 21 orphan databases accumulating in ~/.dolt-data/ (75 MB total), growing with every test run. `gt dolt status` confirms them as orphans.

## Root cause

`beads-test-with-project` macro (beads-test.el:118-151) teardown:
```elisp
(when (file-directory-p ,temp-dir)
  (delete-directory ,temp-dir t))
```

This only cleans the filesystem. The Dolt database created by `beads-command-init` on the server persists.

Same pattern exists in `beads-integration-test.el` with `beads-test--generate-unique-prefix`.

There's also `beads_formula_test_*` orphans from formula tests — same root cause.

## Fix

Add Dolt database teardown to `beads-test-with-project`:

1. Before deleting temp-dir, capture the prefix that was used
2. Issue DROP DATABASE for that prefix via the Dolt SQL connection
3. Something like: `(beads-dolt-query (format "DROP DATABASE IF EXISTS %s" prefix))`
4. Or shell out: `(call-process "bd" nil nil nil "dolt" "drop" prefix)` if bd supports it

Also need to:
- Check if `bd` has a drop/destroy command, or if we need raw SQL
- Apply same fix to integration test helpers
- Consider adding a `beads-test-cleanup-databases` function that can be run manually to clean existing orphans
- Run `gt dolt cleanup` now to clear the 21 existing orphans

## Files

- `lisp/test/beads-test.el` — `beads-test-with-project` macro teardown
- `lisp/test/beads-integration-test.el` — same pattern
- Any formula test files creating `beads_formula_test_*` databases
title: Test teardown leaks Dolt databases — beadsTest* orphans accumulate

## Goal

Open Emacs in a tmux session, run M-x beads, and verify every key binding in the main transient menu opens its sub-command or sub-transient without error (no 'not defined or autoloaded', no void-function errors).

## How to test

1. Start a tmux session
2. Launch Emacs with the beads load-path:
   emacs -Q -L ~/workspace/beads.el/lisp -l beads
   (or use the user's normal Emacs startup: emacs --no-desktop)
3. M-x beads RET  — main menu should open
4. For each key in the transient menu, press it and verify:
   - Sub-transients open (no error)
   - Interactive commands invoke without void-function error
   - Press q or C-g to return between keys
5. Pay special attention to keys that invoke transient sub-prefixes (these are the ones that can fail with 'Suffix command X is not defined or autoloaded'):
   l (beads-list), c (beads-create), s (beads-show), u (beads-update), x (beads-close),
   d (beads-dep), F (beads-formula-menu), m (beads-mol), g (beads-gate), etc.

## Use tmux skill to drive Emacs

Use the tmux tool to:
- Create a new tmux window/pane
- Launch Emacs
- Send keystrokes to navigate the transient menus
- Capture output to check for errors

## Pass criteria

Every key in the main beads transient menu either:
- Opens a sub-transient successfully, OR
- Prompts the user for input (minibuffer), OR
- Shows a buffer with results
No 'void-function', 'not defined or autoloaded', or similar errors.

## Fix any issues found

If errors are found (e.g. missing autoloads, missing requires), fix them in ~/workspace/beads.el/lisp/ and re-test until clean.
Commit fixes to a branch and push for merge.

## Repo

~/workspace/beads.el (same as /home/roman/gt/beads_el/mayor/rig, but this is the path Emacs loads from)
